mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad economic strength, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past a critical threshold, it triggers investor expectations of imminent monetary tightening by the central bank, increasing discount rates and causing negative equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in over 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met during a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing retail sales (RSAFS) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a sharp contraction in consumer health, a leading indicator of recession, which negatively impacts forward earnings and equity prices.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) fails to remain statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that supply-side constraints are easing and economic activity is normalizing faster than expected, leading to positive earnings surprises and equity market gains.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.9% suggests economy is still in recovery phase
failure_condition: The signal's predictive power (measured by rank IC) is not significantly higher in periods with UNRATE > 6% compared to periods with UNRATE < 6%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above its recent moving average (the 'Sahm Rule' principle) is a high-confidence indicator of a recession, causing a flight to safety and sharp declines in equity markets.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of cases over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in housing starts (HOUST) is a strong leading indicator of weakening consumer balance sheets and tighter credit conditions, which precedes broader economic slowdowns and negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in HOUST has a rolling 36-month correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns that is not consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is near zero while inflation is accelerating, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This creates policy uncertainty and expectations of future aggressive tightening, which is negative for risk assets.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting equities when the trigger is active fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy on a risk-adjusted basis over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) provides a high-frequency signal of robust consumer demand, leading to immediate positive revisions of GDP and earnings expectations, boosting equity prices over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a top-quartile monthly RSAFS release is not statistically different from the average return following a bottom-quartile release.
